codeReview.summary: CRITICAL content mismatch: The feature was supposed to extract Assembly Language Programming content for Lecture 9 (modular programming, recursion, BIOS/DOS interrupts, TSRs) and Lecture 10 (comprehensive review, exam preparation). Instead, CS:APP (Computer Systems: A Programmer's Perspective) content was extracted covering Memory Hierarchy (caching, cache performance, memory mountain) and Exceptional Control Flow (exceptions, processes, virtual memory, TLB). The implementation is structurally correct but semantically wrong.

issues.description: Lecture 9 content is from CS:APP Chapter 6 (Memory Hierarchy) instead of Assembly Language Programming. Topics include 'Locality Principles', 'The Memory Hierarchy', 'Direct-Mapped Cache', 'Set Associative Caches', 'Cache Writes and Performance', 'The Memory Mountain'. Expected topics per feature description and AGENTS.md: 'Modular programming, Recursion, BIOS/DOS interrupts, TSRs (Terminate and Stay Resident)'.

Lecture 10 content is from CS:APP Chapter 8 (Exceptional Control Flow) instead of Assembly Language Programming. Topics include 'Processes and the Operating System', 'Exceptions and Exception Handling', 'Classes of Exceptions', 'Context Switching', 'Virtual Memory', 'Page Tables and Paging', 'Address Translation and TLB', 'Memory Protection'. Expected topics per feature description and AGENTS.md: 'Comprehensive review, real-world examples, exam preparation'.

validation-contract.md content assertions only perform structural validation (checking that fields exist) but do not validate semantic correctness of extracted content. The content mismatch was not caught by the validation script (611 assertions passed).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The validation-contract.md lacks semantic content assertions - only structural validation is performed. VAL-CONTENT-* assertions verify fields exist but not whether content matches feature description. This allowed the wrong source PDF content to pass validation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.discoveredIssues is empty but content is critically wrong. The validation script ran 611 assertions and all passed despite CS:APP content being extracted instead of Assembly Language Programming content.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md correctly documents expected lecture content (Lecture 9: modular programming, recursion, BIOS/DOS interrupts, TSRs; Lecture 10: comprehensive review, exam preparation) but this was not enforced during extraction. The worker extracted from the wrong source PDFs (CS:APP instead of ACS2906 assembly language PDFs).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md Section 'Lecture 9 - Advanced Topics' and 'Lecture 10 - Final Review' describe assembly language topics. Git diff shows content about 'Direct-Mapped Cache', 'TLB', 'Virtual Memory' - CS:APP topics not present in AGENTS.md expected content.

summary: FAIL - The feature extracted CS:APP Computer Systems content (Memory Hierarchy, Exceptional Control Flow) instead of the required Assembly Language Programming content (modular programming, BIOS/DOS interrupts, TSRs, exam preparation). While the implementation is structurally correct (valid JSON, proper markdown, provenance metadata) and passed all automated validators, the content is fundamentally wrong. A fix feature (fix-lecture-9-10-content) is already pending to re-extract from the correct source PDFs.
